agenda_briefs.title: Council to appoint Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for 2026 Burnaby election
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election, scheduled for Saturday, October 17, 2026. The previous appointee is on leave and not expected to return before the election. The appointment is needed to ensure continuity in election planning.

agenda_briefs.title: North Road BIA renewal bylaw heads to council vote — 5-year term, April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the results of petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw. A BIA is a special zone where local businesses pay an extra levy to fund improvements like marketing, events, and street cleaning. The renewal would extend the BIA for another 5 years, from April 1, 2026 to March 31, 2031. A certificate of sufficiency has been filed, meaning enough valid signatures were gathered to move the bylaw forward. Council is set to vote on whether to approve Bylaw #14797.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if approved, your business may be subject to the BIA levy for the next 5 years.

key_dates.label: North Road BIA renewal term begins
North Road BIA renewal term ends

agenda_briefs.title: BHA 2025 audited financials and 2026 budget — $2M operating funding contingent on receipt
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider receiving the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) 2025 audited financial statements and 2026 operating budget. Receipt of these documents satisfies conditions for the City to provide $2.00 million in 2026 operating funding under Partnering Agreement No. 1. The BHA reported an accumulated surplus of $7.52 million as of Dec 31, 2025, with $31.75 million in cash. KPMG issued a clean audit opinion.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby e-bike share pilot: parking zones, speed rules, operator fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider amendments to three bylaws to launch an e-bike share pilot and address the provincial e-scooter pilot. Key changes: shared e-bikes and e-scooters must park only in designated Shared Micromobility Parking Zones; e-scooters are banned from major roads and arterials except in protected cycle lanes or multi-use pathways; operators must have a valid City agreement and pay fees based on device count and usage. Violations of moving-vehicle rules are enforced by RCMP under the Motor Vehicle Act.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council decision — if approved, businesses near proposed parking zones may see changes in sidewalk and street use, and potential operators must prepare to apply for a City agreement.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to consider bylaw amendments

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby housing target progress report: 910 net new units in first 6 months
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing submission of Burnaby's Housing Target Progress Report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act. The report covers September 1, 2025 to February 28, 2026, showing 910 net new housing units completed (1,097 completions minus 187 demolitions) against a Year 1 target of 1,536 units. The city must submit the report by resolution within 45 days of the reporting period end (deadline April 14, 2026). The five-year target is 10,240 net new units by August 31, 2030, with annual cumulative targets of 1,536 (Year 1), 3,174 (Year 2), 5,069 (Year 3), 7,373 (Year 4), and 10,240 (Year 5).

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby to join Building Benchmark BC Year 7 — no new fees
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider authorizing the City of Burnaby to participate in Year 7 of the Building Benchmark BC program, a voluntary program where large buildings (over 50,000 sq ft) track and report energy and water use. The city already includes 30 civic buildings. No additional funding is required beyond the existing 2026 budget. The decision has not been made yet — council is set to vote on this at an upcoming meeting.

agenda_briefs.title: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain for Mintara restaurant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use specific land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way on Burnaby Mountain. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, including the Mintara restaurant. The right-of-way also covers an existing gas main from the 1960s that currently lacks legal protection. No compensation is being paid by FortisBC. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.

agenda_briefs.title: FortisBC natural gas right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain for Mintara restaurant
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider granting a Statutory Right-of-Way (a legal permission to use specific land) to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way on Burnaby Mountain. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, including the Mintara restaurant. The right-of-way also covers an existing gas main from the 1960s that currently lacks legal protection. No compensation is being paid by FortisBC. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M modular four-plex pilot at 8315 14th Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue. The three-storey, 5,319 sq ft building will include four affordable units (one accessible) with private outdoor space. The project is supported by $150,000 from Fortis BC and $200,000 from the National Research Council. Construction is expected to take 6–8 months, with a 50–60 year useful life. The City will own the land and building, leasing units through its rental portfolio.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — if approved, this pilot could lead to more modular housing projects in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Council vote on modular housing pilot contract award

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. for the Jerry Rogers Creek Culvert Replacement Project. The work includes installing 45 lineal meters of 1.8m x 1.8m box culvert, 40 lineal meters of DI watermain, concrete storm pipe tie-ins, and replacement of a Metro Vancouver sanitary main. The new culvert is designed to meet 100-year climate change flows.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 20-storey, 215-unit rental rezoning at 5035 Beresford Street
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to forward a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) for a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The development would include 215 market rental units (32 studio, 98 one-bedroom, 65 two-bedroom, 20 three-bedroom), 38 residential vehicle parking spaces (100% EV ready), 441 secured bicycle spaces, and 574.74 m² outdoor rooftop amenity area. The site is currently zoned M4 Special Industrial and would be rezoned to CD Comprehensive Development District. This is the first step in the approval process — if Council agrees, the application will move to 'First and Second Reading' (a formal review stage) at a future meeting. No final decision has been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.

key_dates.label: Council vote to forward rezoning to First and Second Reading

agenda_briefs.title: Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth — final recommendations due June 23, 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council received an interim update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. The task force has identified strategic priorities and is validating them through a business community survey and 10 stakeholder interviews conducted March 13–April 2, 2026. The task force will reconvene April 16, 2026 for three additional sessions through end of May to finalize recommendations, which will be presented to Council on June 23, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Developer forfeits $1,363,500 security for public pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court
agenda_briefs.summary: Council received a report that Oxford Properties forfeited a $1,363,500 security deposit instead of building the public amenity pier at 8351 Fraser Reach Court, part of the Riverbend Business Park rezoning. The City now holds the funds and may use them for the pier or an alternative amenity along the Fraser Foreshore or elsewhere in Burnaby. The covenant will be discharged from title once the forfeited security is received.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action required — watch for future Council direction on how the forfeited $1,363,500 will be used.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw vote — 5-year levy up to $385,740/year
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on Bylaw 14797 to create a Business Improvement Area (BIA) along Burnaby North Road. If passed, owners of Class 5 (light industry) and Class 6 (business/other) properties in the BIA will pay a local service tax based on assessed value to fund the Association. The total grant over the 5-year term (April 1, 2026 – March 31, 2031) is $1,755,147.81, with annual amounts ranging from $317,954.94 (Year 1) to $385,740.60 (Year 5).
agenda_briefs.action_required: Contact your council representative before the vote if you oppose the BIA levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw public hearing / council vote
BIA term begins — levy applies to eligible properties
BIA term ends

minutes_briefs.title: Council appointed Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for 2026 election
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously approved the appointment of Dennis Back as the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election. This means Mr. Back will be responsible for overseeing and managing the municipal election process in Burnaby in 2026, ensuring it runs fairly and according to the rules.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.1 APPOINTMENT OF CHIEF ELECTION OFFICER

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report recommending the appointment of the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 165
THAT Dennis Back be appointed Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved Certificate of Sufficiency for North Road BIA renewal
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has unanimously approved the Certificate of Sufficiency for the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal. This means the petitions submitted against the proposed BIA renewal bylaw did not meet the required threshold to stop the process. The BIA will now move forward with a 5-year renewal. A BIA is a local business association that collects a levy from businesses in the area to fund improvements like marketing, beautification, and events.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No action required from your business at this time. The BIA renewal process is moving forward as planned.
minutes_briefs.industries: Retail, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt
minutes_briefs.source_body:

The General Manager Corporate Services submitted a report providing results for the petitions submitted against the proposed North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) renewal bylaw for a 5-year period.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 166
THAT the report titled “Certificate of Sufficiency: North Road BIA Renewal” dated April 14, 2026 be received for information;
THAT the Certificate of Sufficiency dated April 14, 2026, certified by the Corporate Officer and included as Attachment 1 to this report, be received; and
THAT a copy of this report be sent to the President of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Association.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved annual corporate maintenance matters for Burnaby Housing Authority for 2026
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council, as the sole shareholder of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA), approved the 2026 annual corporate maintenance matters. Council authorized the annual general meeting to be conducted through a unanimous shareholders resolution, set December 12, 2026 as the annual reference date, appointed KPMG as auditor until the next annual general meeting, received the BHA's financial statements for the year ending December 31, 2025, and appointed the directors listed in the report. The Shareholder Representative was directed to sign any necessary resolutions to carry out these decisions.

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved bylaw amendments for e-bike share and e-scooter pilots
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to authorize amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw, the Parks Regulation Bylaw, and the Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw. These changes allow the City to run an E-Bike Share Pilot Program and to address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program. The decision is final and the City Solicitor has been directed to bring forward the necessary bylaw changes.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required from business owners. Monitor City of Burnaby communications for when the pilot programs launch and any new rules for e-bikes and e-scooters.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.5 PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS TO ADDRESS E-BIKE SHARE PILOT PROGRAM AND PROVINCIAL ELECTRIC KICK SCOOTER PILOT PROGRAM

The General Manager Engineering submitted a report proposing amendments to the Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw and the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program within the City.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 169
THAT the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward amendments to the following bylaws to implement the E-Bike Share Pilot Program and address the provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program:
• Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961;
• Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979; and
• Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,

as described in the report titled “Proposed Bylaw Amendments to Address E-Bike Share Pilot Program and Provincial Electric Kick Scooter Pilot Program”, dated April 14, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved submission of Burnaby's housing progress report to BC
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved and submitted its 6-month interim progress report on the Provincial Housing Target Order to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. This report, required under the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation (provincial laws that set housing construction targets for municipalities), shows how Burnaby is meeting its provincially-set housing goals. Council voted unanimously to receive the report and authorize its submission.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
7.6 PROVINCIAL HOUSING TARGET ORDER 6-MONTH INTERIM PROGRESS REPORT

The General Manager Planning and Development submitted a report presenting the Housing Target Order and obtain Council authorization to forward Burnaby’s Housing Target Progress Report Form to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs, as required by the Housing Supply Act and Housing Supply Regulation.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 170
THAT the report titled “Provincial Housing Target Order 6-Month Interim Progress Report”, dated April 14, 2026, be received for information; and
THAT the General Manager Planning and Development be authorized to submit the Housing Target Progress Report Form, as found in Attachment 2, to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved a FortisBC gas line right-of-way on Burnaby Mountain
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved granting a Statutory Right-of-Way to FortisBC Energy Inc. over parts of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This right-of-way is a legal permission allowing FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas pipes and equipment needed to service the Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. The decision was unanimous.
minutes_briefs.source_body:

The General Manager Lands and Facilities submitted a report seeking Council approval to grant Statutory Right-of-Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. (FortisBC) over a portion of 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way to permit the installation, operations and maintenance of natural gas works.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 172
THAT the granting and execution of Statutory Right-of-Way over a portion of 7950 Pandora St. and 1200 Gaglardi Way in favour of FortisBC Energy Inc. for the installation, operations, and maintenance of infrastructure required to service Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area, as outlined in the report titled “7950 Pandora St & 1200 Gaglardi Way – Burnaby Mountain – Statutory Right-of-Way for FortisBC”, dated April 14, 2026, be approved.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved hiring external reviewer to assess city finances
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to hire an independent expert to review the city's financial planning, spending practices, and how it sets priorities for big projects like roads and buildings. The goal is to protect the city's money, avoid big tax hikes, and keep costs down for businesses and residents. The reviewer will work with the Financial Management Committee and report directly to Council.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.1 FMC - EXTERNAL REVIEW OF CITY FINANCIAL PRACTICES

The Financial Management Committee (FMC) submitted a report seeking Council approval to engage an external reviewer to help safeguard the City's assets and financial standing, and to mitigate the risk of significant tax increases and rising service costs for businesses and residents.
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 181
THAT engaging a qualified external reviewer to assess the City’s financial planning, operating practices, and capital investment priority setting, as discussed at the Financial Management Committee meeting held on March 17, 2026, be approved; and
THAT the reviewer liaise with the Financial Management Committee and deliver findings and recommendations directly to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council approved rezoning for 20-storey apartment at 5035 Beresford Street
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to advance a rezoning bylaw (REZ #25-11) for a 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street. The project will include 215 residential units above underground parking. Council also authorized a Housing Agreement, if needed, and set conditions that must be met before the bylaw gets final approval. This decision moves the project forward to first and second reading at a future council meeting.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
8.2 PDC - REZ #25-11 - 5035 BERESFORD STREET - PROPOSED 20-STOREY APARTMENT DEVELOPMENT (ROYAL OAK COMMUNITY PLAN)

The Planning and Development Committee (PDC) submitted a report seeking Council authorization to forward REZ #25-11 to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting. The purpose of the proposed zoning bylaw amendment is to permit the construction of a 20-storey multiple-family apartment development comprised of 215 residential units above underground parking.  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 182
THAT a Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11 be prepared and advanced to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting;
THAT a Housing Agreement, if required, be authorized according to the terms outlined in Section 3.4 of the report titled “REZ #25-11 - 5035 Beresford Street – Proposed 20-Storey Apartment Development (Royal Oak Community Plan)”, dated March 31, 2026, of the Planning and Development Committee meeting, and the City Solicitor be authorized to bring forward a Housing Agreement Bylaw; and
THAT the items listed in Attachment 3 to the report be established as prerequisites to consideration of Final Adoption of the Rezoning Bylaw for REZ #25-11.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Council abandoned Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw after petitions
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council voted unanimously to abandon the proposed Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026. This means the bylaw will not go ahead. The decision was made because enough property owners signed petitions against it, meeting the legal threshold required to stop the process.
minutes_briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
minutes_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1 #14797 - Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026
Reminder regarding the petition related - 7.2 the petitioner has met the legislative threshold - bylaw will be abandoned  
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2026 - 185
THAT due to sufficient petitions received by the City against Burnaby Business Improvement Area (Burnaby North Road) Bylaw 2026, Bylaw No. 14797 be now abandoned.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council refers CFIB letter on PST expansion to staff for review
minutes_briefs.summary: Council received a letter from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business (CFIB) urging the City to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax (PST) expansion on professional services. Council voted unanimously to refer the letter to staff for further review and to report back. No decision has been made yet on whether Burnaby will take a formal position against the PST expansion.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
12.1 Item 3.1 Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package Dated April 14, 2026

Councillor Lee referred to Item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submission Package from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax expansion on professional services.


Arising from discussion, the following motion was brought forward:
MOVED AND SECONDED
Resolution No. 2025 - 186
THAT item 3.1 of the Council Correspondence and Public Submission Package dated April 14, 2026, from the Canadian Federation of Independent Business urging Council to oppose the Provincial Sales Tax Expansion on professional service, be REFERRED to staff for further review and report back to Council.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby Council approved 2025 Annual Municipal Report and opened it for public comment
minutes_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the 2025 Annual Municipal Report, which includes the city's audited financial statements for the year ending December 31, 2025. The report and a highlights document are now available for public review and comment starting April 15, 2026. This is a routine annual reporting requirement that gives residents and business owners a chance to see how the city spent money and managed operations last year.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Review the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and Highlights on the city's website and submit any comments before the comment period closes.

key_dates.label: Public comment period opens for 2025 Annual Municipal Report

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Resolution No. C2026 - 036
THAT the report titled “2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights”, dated for April 7, 2026, of the Audit Committee meeting, be received for information;
THAT the 2025 Annual Municipal Report (Attachment 1), which includes the audited Consolidated Financial Statements, for the year ended December 31, 2025, be approved; and
THAT the 2025 Annual Municipal Report and 2025 Annual Municipal Report Highlights documents be made available to the public for commentary starting on April 15, 2026.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY
